spec_match.rationale: Reads `screenPixelRatio` from the captured track and uses it to size a canvas at the physical pixel count for a 1:1 remote-desktop render, with a blurry-vs-sharp comparison. That is the high-DPR consumer scenario the spec calls out.

interactive.rationale: Start-capture button + 1 main listener drives both render passes (DPR-ignored vs DPR-aware) live; feature-detect probe at the top.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `Side-by-side shows blurry (DPR ignored) vs sharp (DPR applied) ...`. Page is exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Two-render comparison shows the win on a HiDPI display but doesn't degrade gracefully on a 1x display where the comparison is meaningless, nor demonstrate the case where the captured surface DPR differs from `window.devicePixelRatio`.

references.rationale: Page has no `see also` / `references` section at all (sections list is empty). Missing the required `https://chromestatus.com/feature/6738236535472128` link for invariant #3.

design_system.rationale: Inline styles use raw hex (`#f8f8f0`, `#e2e8f0`, `#1e293b`, `#64748b`) bypassing the variable palette in `public/styles.css`. Invariant #5 only permits the defined variables.

openQuestions.title: Add a references section with chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: Page is missing `see also`/`references` entirely. Invariant #3: every concept page must cite `https://chromestatus.com/feature/6738236535472128` so the slug-recovery script can self-heal. Add the section.

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex with design-system variables
openQuestions.detail: Inline styles use `#f8f8f0`, `#e2e8f0`, `#1e293b`, `#64748b` — not in the design system. Map each to its closest `--bg-*` / `--text-*` variable from `public/styles.css`.

openQuestions.title: Handle 1x-display visitors
openQuestions.detail: On a 1x monitor both renders look identical. Detect that and show a banner explaining the demo is HiDPI-specific, plus suggest the user view it on a 2x display.

openQuestions.title: Highlight surface-DPR vs window-DPR divergence
openQuestions.detail: If the captured surface is on a different display than the viewer's window, the two DPRs may differ. Surface both side-by-side so authors see they can't shortcut by reading `window.devicePixelRatio`.

summary: Right shape — uses screenPixelRatio to size a remote-desktop canvas for 1:1 fidelity — but ships missing the references section and using a non-system hex palette. Both are required-by-CLAUDE.md fixes.
